name: alpha-mangostin
summary: Major xanthone from mangosteen pericarp with documented NF-κB suppression and antioxidant activity. Strong preclinical profile; limited human bioavailability and clinical data.
description: Major xanthone from mangosteen pericarp with documented NF-κB suppression and antioxidant activity. Strong preclinical profile; limited human bioavailability and clinical data.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: Use cautious, source-specific language; do not generalize beyond the cited population/preparation.
claimMap.notes: Use cautious, source-specific language; do not generalize beyond the cited population/preparation.

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: Pharmacokinetics of α-mangostin in rats after intravenous and oral application.
sources.citation: Author not retrieved (2011). Mol Nutr Food Res. PMID: 21254395. DOI: 10.1002/mnfr.201000511.
sources.note: source-backed gap-fill audit only; not a full monograph field extraction

contraindications: existing liver disease warrants clinician guidance due to limited human hepatic safety data
warfarin or other anticoagulant medications or phenytoin or NSAIDs due to demonstrated in-vitro CYP2C8/CYP2C9 enzyme inhibition and elevated bleeding risk
cardiovascular disease or blood pressure medications warrant clinician guidance due to documented cardiovascular activity
pregnancy or breastfeeding due to insufficient human safety data
diabetes medications may require monitoring due to a theoretical additive blood-glucose-lowering effect from related mangosteen xanthones
